summary.narrative: Ferguson delivered on a majority of his marquee campaign commitments this term: the $100M local police-hiring grant program (HB 2015), the ~$20M-plus Washington State Ferries crew investment, CHIP affordable-housing infrastructure grants, historic Housing Trust Fund investments, reproductive-health protections, and enactment of the Millionaires' Tax (SB 6346). The 200,000-housing-unit goal shows early executive and budget action but no documented progress toward the numeric target. Two priorities moved against the promised direction or stalled: universal free school lunch was scaled back before being partially funded via the tax law, and Working Connections childcare faced proposed cuts rather than the promised $100M expansion.
summary.grade_rationale: The record shows several fully delivered, high-clarity commitments backed by signed legislation and budget appropriations, alongside one large quantitative goal with only early action and two commitments (childcare expansion, school lunch) that contracted in scope or reversed direction. The pattern is strong delivery on legislatively achievable priorities the governor drove, weaker performance on the largest multi-year structural target and on commitments squeezed by budget shortfalls.

2026-03-27: Signed SB 6026 allowing housing in commercial/mixed-use zones (c126).
2026-04-01: Stated total biennium affordable housing investment reached $960M (c101).

data_gaps: No prior terms in seat. Several delivery sources are Tier-3 opinion/conservative outlets (seattlered, mynorthwest) for the tax-promise contestation — used cautiously and flagged CONTESTED. The 200,000-unit promise lacks any unit-count tracking evidence (LIMITED EVIDENCE). The WSP troopers commitment has no located delivery evidence beyond the priorities page. Working Connections classified D0/REVERSED based on multiple reports of proposed cuts; reviewer should confirm final enacted figures. Free-school-lunch funding via SB 6346 is contested as non-enforceable (c181).

provisional_status.display: Grade is record-to-date — term in progress, 35.8% elapsed (as of 2026-06-22). It will change as the term continues.
provisional_status.note: Distinct from any per-promise 'PROVISIONAL (early-term)' flag in scorecard.card_flags, which concerns a single promise's own deadline, not the term's completeness.
